provenance.vendored_source: https://github.com/eyereasoner/eye (reasoning/socrates, reasoning/path, reasoning/dog, reasoning/snaf, reasoning/deep-taxonomy, reasoning/nixon-diamond, reasoning/backward, reasoning/good-cobbler, reasoning/peano, reasoning/skos, reasoning/filter, reasoning/basic-monadic, reasoning/edt)
provenance.vendored_description: The `vendored` suite reproduces EYE reasoner community test cases, lightly adapted where noted in each .n3 file's header comment. 5 cases (socrates, path, dog, snaf, deep-taxonomy [truncated from a 10000-level chain to 3 levels]) run unmodified (or near-unmodified) against this engine's supported subset and are 'active'. 6 more (nixon-diamond, backward, good-cobbler, peano, skos, filter) are vendored with `status: ignored` and a precise `blocked_reason` documenting the exact missing feature (rule-priority ordering, goal-directed/backward query evaluation, general list-term pattern unification, denial/`=> false` rule heads, `func:`/`string:lessThan` builtins). basic-monadic and edt are documentation-only stubs (no runnable _result.n3) noting why they're redundant-at-scale or need the same list-unification gap. Everything in `syntax` and hand-authored `builtins` cases is authored for this crate directly.

runner_semantics: For every active case, the runner loads `input` via TripleStore::from, calls materialize(), decodes every inferred triple to its 'S P O.' string form, and asserts every triple listed in `expected` is present among them (a subset/entailment check, not exact equality -- both because forward-chaining fixpoints often derive more than any one hand-picked expectation, and because two of the vendored EYE cases were themselves only checked by EYE against a narrow, non-generic query).

test_cases.name: log:collectAllIn distinct-value collection into an RDF list
Symmetric-transitive path closure over a road graph (<= sugar)
EYE 'dog' corpus case: log:collectAllIn + n-ary math:sum + math:greaterThan
EYE 'snaf' corpus case: log:notIncludes SNAF guard
EYE 'deep-taxonomy' corpus case (truncated to a 3-level chain): rdfs:subClassOf backward-chaining transitivity
EYE 'nixon-diamond' corpus case: rule-priority-dependent SNAF guard
test_cases.blocked_reason: Reasoner::materialize evaluates every rule in a stratum against one shared per-iteration TripleIndex snapshot, batch-merging all newly-derived triples only after the whole iteration finishes -- there is no rule-priority/ordering concept, so Rule 1's log:notIncludes guard can never observe Rule 2's NonPacifist fact within the same iteration. Both ex:Pacifist and ex:NonPacifist get derived instead of only ex:NonPacifist. See the file's own header comment for the full explanation.

test_cases.name: EYE 'backward' corpus case: goal-directed math:greaterThan proof
test_cases.blocked_reason: EYE proves this goal-directed, seeding the backward rule with the concrete query '5 :moreInterestingThan 3' and working backward to check '5 math:greaterThan 3'. This engine is purely forward-chaining with no query/goal-injection mechanism, so '?X math:greaterThan ?Y' with no facts providing candidate X/Y values never binds anything.

test_cases.name: EYE 'good-cobbler' corpus case: list-term pattern unification
test_cases.blocked_reason: List terms are opaque synthetic blank-node ids (see triples.rs LIST_REGISTRY); TripleIndex::query only compares such ids for exact equality and does no structural/element-wise list-pattern unification, so a rule pattern like (:good ?Y) can never match a separately-parsed ground list (:good :Cobbler) even though they're structurally compatible.

test_cases.name: EYE 'peano' corpus case: nested list-term backward-chained arithmetic
test_cases.blocked_reason: Requires structural unification of nested list-term patterns (e.g. (?A (:s ?B)) against derived nested list values), recursively, driven backward from a query goal. This engine has neither general list-pattern unification (see vendored_good_cobbler) nor backward/goal-directed evaluation; peano needs both, compounded by recursion. Completely out of scope.

test_cases.name: EYE 'skos' corpus case: SKOS rules with denial (=> false) heads
test_cases.blocked_reason: Not vendored as a runnable case (no _result.n3 sibling exists; see the header comment in vendored/skos.n3). Beyond the ordinary forward-chaining SKOS rules this engine already handles, skos-rules.n3 requires a bodyless '=> false.' denial-rule head (Rule.head: Triple has no boolean/denial variant), ';'-shared-subject predicate-object-list syntax, and the func: RIF-builtin-function namespace (func:lang-from-PlainLiteral), none of which this ticket's scope covers.

test_cases.name: EYE 'filter' corpus case: log:collectAllIn combined with string:lessThan
test_cases.blocked_reason: Not vendored as a runnable case (no _result.n3 sibling exists; see the header comment in vendored/filter.n3). Its collectAllIn use is otherwise exactly what this ticket's log:collectAllIn implementation supports, but the embedded formula filters via string:lessThan, a string: builtin not yet implemented (only string:length/string:concat exist).

test_cases.name: EYE 'basic-monadic' corpus case: cyclic list-term pattern + generated benchmark data
test_cases.blocked_reason: Not vendored as a runnable case (no _result.n3 sibling exists; see the header comment in vendored/basic_monadic.n3). Needs the same list-term structural unification good_cobbler/peano lack (here to detect a repeated variable closing an 11-element cycle list), and its fixture data is randomly-generated 10^n-scale benchmark data rather than a small fact set.

test_cases.name: EYE 'edt' corpus case: duplicate of deep-taxonomy at unpractical scale
test_cases.blocked_reason: Not vendored as a runnable case (no _result.n3 sibling exists; see the header comment in vendored/edt.n3). Exercises the exact same rdfs:subClassOf backward rule as vendored_deep_taxonomy (which does run, truncated) over a 30002-triple, 10000-level chain -- a scale/perf duplicate, not a new feature.
